safe_claim: Current Linux workqueue source anchors are mapped as generic async surfaces that require explicit typed CapSched carriers for Domain-originated work; generic workqueue state is not authority.
forbidden_claim: Generic workqueue pending state, worker identity, callback identity, flush/cancel state, or queue membership is caller Domain authority, monitor receipt proof, runtime coverage, behavior approval, or production protection.

source_rows.candidate_role: Raw generic work item identity; must not carry caller authority by itself.
source_rows.authority_boundary: work_struct address and embedded data bits are not a typed CapSched carrier.
source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: work_struct pointer as caller Domain authority
source_rows.next_step: future Domain-originated work needs an explicit wrapper or container with immutable carrier fields

source_rows.candidate_role: Callback type for generic work execution.
source_rows.authority_boundary: callback function pointer is execution code identity, not caller provenance.
source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: work_func_t or work->func as caller authority
source_rows.next_step: typed carrier retrieval must be separate from callback identity

source_rows.candidate_role: Defines generic pending state used by queue_work coalescing.
source_rows.authority_boundary: WORK_STRUCT_PENDING is queue state, not caller-specific receipt storage.
source_rows.next_step: carrier preservation must be modeled separately from pending-bit state

source_rows.authority_boundary: work_pending() cannot identify which caller owns the pending carrier.
source_rows.next_step: typed carrier validity needs caller/ticket/receipt/generation fields

source_rows.candidate_role: Public wrapper to queue work on an unbound CPU target.
source_rows.authority_boundary: queue_work() return value is scheduling state only, not receipt admission.
source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: queue_work success or failure as monitor receipt
source_rows.next_step: document queue_work false behavior for carrier preservation

source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: schedule_work caller context as preserved Domain authority
source_rows.next_step: Domain-originated async work must use a typed submission wrapper

source_rows.candidate_role: Documents generic queued-state encoding in work->data.
source_rows.authority_boundary: pool/workqueue bookkeeping is not caller authority.
source_rows.next_step: carrier fields must live outside generic queued-state bits

source_rows.candidate_role: Clears pending state before callback execution.
source_rows.authority_boundary: clearing pending does not prove monitor revoke or carrier freshness.
source_rows.next_step: stale carrier rejection must be explicit before worker callback side effects

source_rows.symbol_or_pattern: static bool assign_work(struct work_struct *work, struct worker *worker,
source_rows.candidate_role: Assigns generic work to a worker.
source_rows.authority_boundary: worker assignment is not caller authority transfer.
source_rows.next_step: typed carrier must be checked separately from worker assignment

source_rows.symbol_or_pattern: static void insert_work(struct pool_workqueue *pwq, struct work_struct *work,
source_rows.candidate_role: Inserts generic work into pool or inactive list.
source_rows.authority_boundary: pool insertion does not create monitor receipt authority.
source_rows.next_step: Domain-originated carrier enqueue must be modeled before insertion side effects are relevant

source_rows.candidate_role: Core generic queue path; sees many kernel-internal work items.
source_rows.authority_boundary: __queue_work() must not be hooked as if every work item is Domain-originated.
source_rows.next_step: source-map Domain-originated wrappers before any workqueue code proposal

source_rows.candidate_role: Sets WORK_STRUCT_PENDING and returns false for already pending work.
source_rows.authority_boundary: queue_work_on() false cannot authorize overwriting caller-specific carrier fields.
source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: queue_work_on false path as permission to replace BudgetTicket or receipt
source_rows.next_step: carrier design must preserve first caller/ticket/receipt/generation under coalescing

source_rows.candidate_role: Can modify or queue delayed work and interacts with pending state.
source_rows.authority_boundary: delay modification is not carrier replacement authority.
source_rows.next_step: future typed carrier design must decide whether delayed-work retiming preserves, rejects, or regenerates carrier state

source_rows.symbol_or_pattern: static void process_one_work(struct worker *worker, struct work_struct *work)
source_rows.candidate_role: Worker-side execution edge for generic work.
source_rows.authority_boundary: worker execution context is not caller Domain authority.
source_rows.next_step: typed carrier check must be opt-in for Domain-originated work, not inferred globally

source_rows.candidate_role: Actual callback call in worker context.
source_rows.authority_boundary: callback invocation must not execute caller side effects unless a typed carrier has already passed intersection and freshness checks.
source_rows.forbidden_upgrade: current_func invocation as authority-preserving caller execution
source_rows.next_step: future wrapper should enforce service/caller intersection before callback side effects

source_rows.candidate_role: Worker object tracks task/current work/current function state.
source_rows.authority_boundary: worker object identity is not caller Domain authority.
source_rows.next_step: do not store caller authority in generic worker identity

source_rows.authority_boundary: current_wq_worker() is execution context, not provenance.
source_rows.next_step: caller Domain must come from typed carrier, not current worker

source_rows.authority_boundary: flush waits for work state; it is not a monitor revoke receipt.
source_rows.next_step: revoke completion must remain monitor-owned and generation-aware

source_rows.authority_boundary: cancel synchronization is not stale-carrier generation invalidation by itself.
source_rows.next_step: future carrier revoke must cover queued, running, requeued, and freed states
